title: Missing Validation for Identical Tokens
reason: `deposit()` and `withdraw()` require `factory.getPair(token0, token1) != address(0)`, so identical-token inputs are rejected unless the factory itself exposes such a pair.

title: Potential Integer Overflow in `_calculateSwapAmount`
reason: Solidity 0.8 reverts on arithmetic overflow, and realistic reserve/input sizes are far below the range needed to make this a practical protocol issue.

title: Operators Can Withdraw from Any User's Basket
reason: The nonzero-`basketId` branch only lets operators force a withdrawal back to the same `account`; it does not redirect funds. Any operator theft is already more directly covered by `withdrawToken`.

title: No Deadline Check on Router Interactions
reason: Passing `block.timestamp` as the router deadline forces same-block execution rather than allowing indefinite pending execution, so the stated issue is backwards.

title: OperatorSetter Role is Irreversible
reason: This is a governance/trust-model concern, not a standalone exploitable vulnerability in the contract logic.

title: Balance Check Before `transferFrom` Allows Token Manipulation
reason: The before/after balance pattern is a standard way to support fee-on-transfer tokens here, and no concrete reentrant accounting exploit is supported by the contract state transitions.

title: Refund Calculation Can Underflow
reason: `addLiquidity()` returns the amounts actually consumed, which are bounded by the desired amounts; the subtraction only reverts if the router breaks its interface guarantees.

title: Unrestricted ETH Receive Function
reason: Accepting unsolicited ETH may create recoverable dust, but by itself it does not create realistic protocol-level harm.

title: Missing Return Value Check on Reward Claim
reason: `IRewarderv2.claim()` is declared with no return value, so there is no unchecked boolean or amount to validate.
